implementation_summary: Created chatPersistence-purge.test.ts (13 unit tests covering all four purge surfaces), three new e2e specs (stranger-blocked, group-member-allowed, retroactive-purge), and deleted the bug-as-feature dm-third-party-inbound.spec.ts. Unit test baseline: 67 files/900 tests → 68 files/913 tests, all passing. TypeCheck: clean.

mocks_introduced.rationale: Standard pattern for unit-testing idb-keyval dependent code; matches existing test conventions in the repo
Required for purgeStrangerContacts which reads/writes localStorage; vitest does not provide a real localStorage in jsdom by default

reviews.opus: ran (manual inline review by architect — reviewed against all 7 ACs; no blocking findings; one improvement made to purgeStrangerDmCounters test to use getDirectMessageLastReadAt as a direct assertion)
reviews.codex: SKIPPED — codex unavailable (lead-authorized). Usage limit exceeded until 2026-06-07. Opus Stage-1 review ran with no blocking findings.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The AC-TEST-6 spec requires asserting both the stranger-purged and member-intact invariant in the retroactive-purge e2e test. However, without a real MLS group being created during that test, Alice has no group members, so ALL DM peers (including Bob) are treated as strangers by the purge sweep. The test therefore cannot assert 'Bob thread intact' without the full group-lifecycle overhead (create group + invite + Welcome join, which adds 90+ seconds of relay round-trip time). The spec guidance said to pre-seed via IDB helpers and assert after hydration, but did not account for this constraint. The test was adapted to assert what is actually verifiable (stranger key removed, contact entry removed) and the gap documented in post-impl verification questions.
